summary: gpt-sdk postinstall exfiltrates host and CI identity to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On npm install, package.json's postinstall script runs an inline `node -e` payload that collects os.hostname(), process.cwd(), and a broad set of CI/source-control identity environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL, SYSTEM_TEAMFOUNDATIONCOLLECTIONURI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, npm_config_registry) and HTTP-GETs them to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping. The transmitted data identifies installers and reveals internal infrastructure topology — Jenkins URLs, internal GitLab/Azure DevOps server hostnames, private npm registry hostnames — over cleartext HTTP to a bare IP. The package's own index.js is an empty `module.exports = {}`, so the postinstall beacon is the package's only effect. The README's framing of this as an anonymous 'honeypot ping' contradicts the actual payload, which is a targeted CI-identity scrape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
